prompt: A breathtaking underwater photograph of a massive, sprawling coral reef. As you look closer, every single piece of 'coral' and 'seaweed' is actually composed of discarded, colorful single-use plastic waste—crumpled plastic bags, crushed water bottles, tangled fishing nets, and food wrappers. The lighting is cinematic, with sun rays piercing down through the water, highlighting the tragic beauty and total synthetic replacement of the ecosystem. Hyper-detailed, photorealistic, National Geographic style. No text, no labels.
insight: More than 130 million metric tons of single-use plastic are thrown away globally each year, drowning our natural ecosystems in an indestructible tide of synthetic waste.
